violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Establishment must register ServSafe Manager with RIDOH. New Violation. Correct By: 07/29/2025

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to increase pest control. Establishment is to clean and sanitize all mouse droppings using appropriate means of sanitation. Establishment is to have pest control reports available to inspector. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2025

The door #3 to the outside, located in the gym, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025
The gasket for milk chest #1 was in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2025
The 3-bay sink was observed leaking at the time of inspection. Establishment must fix leak. New Violation. Correct By: 03/06/2025

The floor under the prep sink, and milk chest area has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings, and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings using appropriate methods to clean and sanitize to monitor new activity. New Violation. Correct By: 02/27/2025

The door to the outside, located in the gym (Door #3), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024
The gaskets for milk chest #1 and milk chest #2 were observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/07/2024

The floor in the cafeteria and behind the book vending machine have an accumulation pest droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Establishment is to continue to routinely inspect, and is to continue with pest control Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/23/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2024

The door to the outside, located in the Door 3, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by droppings. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Corrective Action - Establishment must clean under all kitchen equipment, under all shelving and behind all equipment. Establishment must clean the corners of the cafeteria along with the floors of the cafeteria to remove any and all droppings. The closet in the Cafeteria must be cleaned to remove any and all droppings and expired pests. Propers means of cleaning must be used to remove droppings from all areas. Pest control must be increased - a full evaluation must be completed by licensed pest control company- reports must be present at time of Re-Inspection for Inspector to review. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

The Establishment kitchen, cafeteria, closet hallway has an accumulation of soil residue, droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/14/2024

There is insufficient refrigeration equipment/capacity to maintain a temperature of 41 degrees or below. Refrigeration units were observed filled to capacity. Staff is having to use milk cases for food storage. Corrective Action - Adequate refrigeration must be obtained. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023
The milk case located in the cafeteria is in poor repair. Equipment is to be maintained in good condition. New Violation. Correct By: 06/18/2023
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2023
